result.summary: recommendationengine.googleapis.com exposes a legacy Google Cloud Recommendations AI HTTP API; docs indicate OAuth 2.0 access tokens are supported and prediction calls can also use a registered Google Cloud API key.
result.description: Recommendations AI was Google Cloud’s recommendation service for retail and media use cases. Google’s retail documentation says this older service endpoint remains available but has been superseded by newer Retail / AI Commerce Search APIs.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For server-to-server use, create a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), grant it the needed IAM roles for Recommendations AI, and authenticate with Google Application Default Credentials as described in [Google Cloud authentication](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication). For user-based OAuth, create OAuth client credentials from [APIs & Services > Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) and obtain an access token for Google Cloud APIs.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: Create an API key in [Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials). The Recommendations AI client-library docs note that if you use an API key for predictions, the API key must first be registered with the Recommendations AI API using the API key registry service.
